files.SKILL.md:
# Engineering In A Terminal Environment

The deliverable is what lands on disk and survives objective checks. Explanation is not a substitute for a working artifact at the path the task names.

## Lock the contract before editing

Read the task and any referenced check files together. Extract every binding requirement: output paths, filenames, function signatures, field names, sort order, delimiters, anchor dates, numeric limits, and success conditions. Treat each concrete detail in the task as mandatory.

State the contract in one sentence: what must be true on disk or at runtime for the work to be accepted. Keep every later step aligned to that sentence—not to a plausible interpretation of intent.

## Write to the named path immediately

Put a first version at the exact required path on your second action, before long exploration. A partial file at the correct path still counts; a missing path fails outright. Scratch directories are not delivery locations.

## Follow the strongest available evidence

When sources disagree, prefer the task text, then named check files, then live repository or system state, then older notes. If a remembered pattern conflicts with what you can observe now, trust the observation and re-derive.

Do not invent values you have not verified. Guessed or pattern-extrapolated data fails exact checks the same way a missing file does. If recovery is incomplete, emit only what you proved—partial truth beats confident fiction.

## Each command answers a question

Run commands, read full output, then decide. If interactive tooling stalls, write to a temp file and execute through the shell.

Use the file-writing tool for content containing `$`, backticks, or nested quotes—shell heredocs and echoes often corrupt them.

## Verify by running, not by reading

When check files define `test_*` cases, run the project's test runner from the correct working directory—often `pytest`, not bare `python` on the test module. If a non-pytest driver script exists with CLI flags, run that script the same way the environment will.

Never claim a check passed without executing it. Exit code 0 with empty output often means nothing ran.

After each meaningful change, run the narrowest objective check available, fix failures, rerun, then widen scope. Two failures with the same approach—change mechanism on the third attempt.

For compiled programs: build immediately after writing. Read back generated output and confirm boundaries (first/last bytes, line endings, counts). One wrong byte fails exact equality.

The strongest check available locally may still be weaker than the final gate. Prefer solutions that hold across implementations rather than quirks visible only in one copy.

## Match what was named

Exact paths, field names, formats, and quoted signatures—including `async`, `def`, and `static`—character for character. Numbers, size caps, and literals in the task are requirements, not examples.

If the task specifies a count or numeric range, validate your output against it before stopping. One out-of-range value can abort parsing before behavior is tested.

## Implementation and synthesis work

Reach a runnable candidate quickly: required file, command entrypoint, listening service, or recovery script from the declared starting state. Prefer one complete candidate over scattered fragments.

When output shape matters, produce exactly what was asked—no extra fields or commentary that another process might parse.

For visual or numeric reconstruction, derive parameters from the reference artifact (sample pixels, headers, or stated dimensions)—do not hand-wave gradients or fill missing data from aesthetic guesses.

## Damaged storage and recovery

Copy source files before any recovery attempt. Database engines, journaled stores, and archive tools may alter or truncate sources on open—even read-only opens can trigger side effects.

Work on the copy. Inspect raw bytes and sidecars (journal, WAL, backup, alternate representation). Use format-specific recovery tools before improvising.

Never replace missing records with invented rows inferred from naming patterns or arithmetic progressions when the task implies data was lost from storage—you must recover evidence from the damaged artifact or admit partial recovery.

If encoding is involved, derive transforms from known plaintext at fixed offsets when possible. Write the requested export as soon as a candidate is viable and compare structure, ordering, and completeness against any reference material provided.

## HTML sanitizer and browser behavior

When a task pairs a filter with a browser check, read the filter implementation first. Run the filter locally on a copy, then confirm—preferably with a headless browser—that the required side effect still occurs after filtering.

Blocked obvious vectors (for example `javascript:` in metadata redirects) usually mean you need a mismatch between how the filter parses HTML and how the browser parses it—not a louder version of the same idea. Favor constructs that survive the filter's tree repair yet still execute on load without user clicks. Links and buttons that need a click are weaker than load-time execution.

Iterate: craft → filter → observe in browser → adjust.

## Structured concurrency and interruption

When limiting parallel async work with a cap, use explicit async task handles you can cancel—not only a bare gather over coroutines still waiting in a queue.

On cancellation or keyboard interrupt: cancel every started task, await all of them (including `finally` cleanup) with exceptions absorbed, then re-raise the interrupt. A common failure mode is starting only the first N tasks under the cap, receiving interrupt, and exiting before queued or running work runs cleanup—only two "started" lines and zero "cleaned up" lines is a symptom of this.

Preserve input order when the contract requires it. Honor the concurrency limit with a semaphore or equivalent single gate.

## Services, configuration, and replay scripts

Scripts that must run in a fresh environment should install dependencies, write configuration, start daemons with their own binaries (no init system), and confirm reachability before exit.

Rewrite whole configuration files instead of patching fragile regions. Avoid inserting into a main config with line-oriented editors when brace nesting exists—a `}` match often hits the wrong block.

For web servers, place shared directives (`log_format`, rate-limit zones) inside the `http { }` scope; per-site blocks belong in included server configs. Write full files with a single-quoted heredoc delimiter so shell does not expand `$variables` meant for the daemon. Run syntax test, start the daemon, then request the expected URL or port named in the task.

## Git push-to-deploy setups

Bare repositories need an executable `post-receive` (or equivalent) hook that checks out or copies the pushed tree into the web root the task names. Set hook ownership and mode so the git user can run it. After configuring, exercise clone → commit → push → HTTP fetch of the deployed file—not only static directory checks.

## Log and time-window aggregation

Take anchor dates, time zones, and inclusive/exclusive window boundaries only from the task text. When counting "last N days including today," derive the cutoff from the stated anchor—do not assume calendar shortcuts that differ from the spec. Parse severity labels and bracketed formats exactly as written.

## Binary and secret extraction

Run appropriate static and dynamic tools. Pipe tool output directly to the required path instead of retyping through chat. For secrets in binaries, follow evidence from strings, symbols, and memory layout—do not paste placeholder tokens.

## Operational communication (when the task involves people)

Keep updates concise and grounded in verified facts. Match audience: protect confidential HR, legal, security, compensation, or customer-private details from broad channels. Do not create public work items from clearly private sources.

## Portable learning only

If durable notes help, record transferable process lessons—not memorized IDs, endpoints, payloads, or answers from prior runs. Replace stale notes when new evidence contradicts them.

## Refuse

Do not fabricate unobserved data. Do not claim checks passed without running them. Do not edit check files to force acceptance. Do not exfiltrate private content to inappropriate audiences.

A partial answer at the named path beats silence.
